title: Wisdom, Innovation, Networking
location: The Joy Center
description: Join us for a breakfast format of our Wisdom, Innovation, Networking (WIN) event at the Joy Center with spotlight speaker Kelly Kirk, Director of Sanford Lab Homestake Visitor Center in Lead, South Dakota. Leading the Change Highlighting women “leading the change,” the Women’s History Month theme for 2026 recognizes the ways in which women navigate the complexity of the challenges before them, seeking solutions and inspiring others to collaborate and continue to work towards meaningful change. This Women’s History Month, attendees will explore several stories of women who led change in Black Hills history and discover their lasting impact. In taking the time to pause and celebrate, participants will have the opportunity to reflect and reframe their own leadership stories.

title: Beginning Line Dancing
location: Spearfish High School; 1725 N. Main
description: Here’s your opportunity to pick up a fun new skill that works both your body and mind! Join us for a lively session where you'll try out some of the latest line dances. No partner required—so don’t let that hold you back! Come be a part of the movement. These easy-to-learn steps offer a great workout for everyone, and it's something you can enjoy for years to come. We'll groove to a variety of music and dance styles, so there's something for everyone!. Ages 10 yrs and above welcome! Class size limited.

title: Free Nonprofit Workshop!
location: The Joy Center
description: This free workshop offers a unique opportunity to connect with executive directors, funders, and board members in a welcoming space to learn, share, and exchange ideas. Through interactive panel discussions and small-group table conversations, you’ll hear firsthand experiences and explore how to navigate the real-world challenges nonprofits face today. Lunch is provided

location: Spearfish High School, CTE Center, FACS Classroom 1824 Spirit Lane, Spearfish, SD, 57783

description: Join the Hook – Young Professionals Group for a fun evening of cooking at the CTE Center in the Spearfish High School FACS classroom! Join Head Chef Mark Larson and Sous Chef Ashley Schultz from the beloved Buffalo Jump Steakhouse & Saloon as they share culinary expertise and guide us through delicious recipes. Whether you’re a seasoned cook or just starting out, this interactive class is a great opportunity to sharpen your skills, meet fellow young professionals, and enjoy some tasty treats. Don’t miss out on this delightful evening of food and friendship! LIMITED SPOTS AVAILABLE —— Thank you to The Hook's 2026 sponsors: Black Hills Energy | Blind Guy of the Black Hills | Bluepeak | Coeur Mining | Lighthouse Construction | Platinum Car Brokers | SDN Communications | Gregg Fullerton - State Farm Agent | Sundance State Bank | Wolff's Plumbing & Heating

title: 107th Annual Chamber Banquet & Awards
location: Spearfish Holiday Inn Convention Center
description: 107TH ANNUAL SPEARFISH CHAMBER BANQUET & AWARDS Join us as we celebrate business success throughout the Spearfish community! Our theme this year is "Stars, Stripes & Success". Each year the Chamber Board of Directors honors businesses and community members with a variety of meaningful and deserving awards. They honor businesses for their innovation, celebrate their vision, and publicly appreciate their contributions to the quality of life in our community and at their workplace. The businesses and individuals honored have a positive impact on the Spearfish community from volunteerism to financial donations to protecting the places we love and cherish. 2026 AWARDS Spirit of Spearfish Lifetime Achievement Community Impact Membership Choice TICKETS $45 PER PERSON *must make menu choice when registering, choices below YOUR EVENING 2:00 Silent Auction (Bid Beacon app) 5:00-6:00 Social Hour, Cash Bar 6:00  Dinner 6:30 Program MENU CHOICES Beef - Slow-Roasted Roast Beef with Demi-Glace with roasted red potatoes, and green beans amandine Chicken - Chicken Breast with Bacon Mushroom Cream Sauce, roasted red potato, and green bean amandine Pork - Stuffed Pork Loin with Apples, Bacon, and Gouda Topped with cider Demi, roasted red potato, and green bean amandine * Each choice is served with a dinner salad, dinner roll, and coffee THANK YOU TO OUR BANQUET SPONSORS Ainsworth Benning Construction, Black Hills Energy, Black Hills Federal Credit Union, Bluepeak, The Blind Guy of the Black Hills, Coeur Wharf, Highmark Credit Union, Lighthouse Construction, McGough Construction, Monument Health, NeighborWorks DHR, Pioneer Bank and Trust, Platinum Car Brokers, Quik Signs, SDN Communications, SoDak Title, Sundance State Bank, Security First Bank, and Wolff's Plumbing & Heating

title: Poetry on the Road
location: Spearfish Senior Center 1306 N 10th St Spearfish, SD
description: Poetry on the Road is a series of 2 hour events held throughout SD to help foster the creations & appreciation of poetry. The gatherings are supported by the SD State Poetry Society (SDSPS), the SD Humanities Council, & many local sponsors. This event is free & open to the public. Join us for poetry readings from the SD Poet Laureate, Bruce Roseland, & other SDSPS member poets. There will also be an open mic portion, where attendees can sign up to read their own original poems if they wish. All are welcome- to participate or just to listen.

title: Writer's Workshop
location: Spearfish Senior Center 1306 N 10th St Spearfish
description: Heirlooms: Wisdom Writing Workshop Sat. March. 14th 10AM-12:30PM at the Spearfish Senior Center. Join SDSU Extension Gerontology Field Specialist Leacey Brown and South Dakota Humanities Scholar Molly Barari for a dynamic two and a half hour workshop blending reflection, creativity, and resilience. Through guided writing activities and thoughtful discussion, participants will explore the growth, wisdom, and perspective that come with aging. Brown and Barari foster an engaging learning environment that encourages participants to embrace their lived experiences. The workshop also highlights how writing can support coping during challenging times. Free & open to the public. RSVP by calling Stephanie 605-642-2827 or email sscdirector@rushmore.com

title: HR for Small Businesses - Session 3 - Onboarding & Performance Management
location: Online (Live)
description: You have your LLC, EIN, State Tax License, and now you’re ready to take the next step to grow your business and hire an employee - WONDERFUL! But how do you do THAT?!? Join industry experts Shelly Rose, Coeur Wharf, Jennifer Martini, Board of Directors of Black Hills SHRM, and Synova Nicolaisen, Black Hills HR Consulting, for four practical workshops in the series to kick-start the process of hiring employees. This workshop series is perfect for any small business owner, whether you’re new to having employees or want to make sure you’re compliant and following industry best practices. Onboarding & Performance Management With all the time you've invested in the interviewing and hiring process, you'll want to learn best practices to set your new team member up for success! Now that you have your new employee, you’ll want to establish a healthy and efficient onboarding process and set up a plan for performance management. Learn how to proactively handle performance management with processes that help your business and don’t need to feel like a burden. In this session we will discuss: What you should document How to document Practical tips for managing a great relationship with employees

title: It's More Than Moving
location: Spearfish Senior Center 1306 N 10th St Spearfish, SD
description: Are You or a Loved One Considering a Move? Join Peaceful Pines Senior Living at Spearfish on Monday March 30th 11AM for an informative and supportive presentation, with snacks and refreshments provided at the Spearfish Senior Center to discuss: ?? Rightsizing & decluttering ?? Planning a smooth transition ?? Reducing stress during life changes ?? Supporting seniors & families through relocation ?? Estate sales & home cleanouts ?? Creating a customized moving plan Moving doesn’t have to be overwhelming — learn how to make it manageable, meaningful, and even empowering. Caring Transitions specializes in senior relocation, downsizing, estate sales, and home cleanouts, and providing compassionate, professional support every step of the way. We hope to see you there!

title: Cybersecurity Series 5 - Business Email Compromise
location: Online
description: Business Email Compromise (BEC) is one of the most common cyber scams targeting small businesses. Attackers impersonate owners, vendors, and employees to trick staff into sharing sensitive information, buying gift cards, or sending money. These messages often look legitimate, arrive at the busiest times, and exploit everyday workflows like invoicing, payroll, and vendor payments. This webinar will walk through real-world examples and practical strategies to help you prevent, detect, and respond to BEC.

title: America 250 - The SD Story
location: Spearfish Senior Center 1306 N 10th St Spearfish, SD
description: In honor of America’s 250 years, Karen Holzer will share stories from SD & profiles of some of the people that have lived & experienced the state’s journey during that time period. Fur traders, explorers, soldiers, housewives, businessmen along with so many more have been a part of our history. She will tell how circumstances & choices have influenced their lives & subsequently have impacted South Dakota history. Karen is part of the Historically Speaking Speakers Bureau, sponsored by the West River History Conference.

description: The Spearfish Community Blood Drive is open to the public. All blood types are needed. New blood donors are welcome. Vitalant is the local blood provider to hospitals for patients in need of blood transfusions. Vitalant relies on volunteer blood donors to make sure there is a safe and ample blood supply. Walk ins are welcome but appointments are preferred. Roll up your sleeve and save up to 3 lives!

title: Business Reboot
location: 1200 University St. BHSU Student Union 205: Crow Peak Conference Room, Spearfish SD 57799-0001
description: Training Topics: Business Plan, Business Start-up/Preplanning, Managing a Business, Rural Business Devel. Description: This one-day course provides participants with an overview of business fundamentals, while introducing techniques for evaluating the feasibility of business concepts. This class is perfect for those interested in exploring business ownership or other self-employment opportunities by leading participants through the key steps for evaluating business concepts and providing foundational knowledge required to develop a business plan. Participants are introduced to a broad spectrum of entrepreneurial business concepts and resources available to access start-up capital, technical assistance, contracting opportunities, and more. While portions of the training have a military focus, we encourage anyone interested in learning the fundamentals of business ownership to take the course.

title: Body in Balance: Fall Prevention Seminar
location: Spearfish Senior Center 1306 N 10th St Spearfish, SD 57783
description: Body in Balance: A Fall Prevention Seminar on Monday, April 27th 11AM-12PM. Join us for a lunch-and-learn presentation on fall prevention with Dr. Britt Wickett of Elevate Physical Therapy. He will cover fall statistics and safety precautions, and offer you exercise recommendations based on your individual risk. Please RSVP with Sam by calling her at 605.340.0098 or emailing her at samantha.wager@hmecare.net. Located at the Spearfish Senior Center 1306 N 10th St. Spearfish, SD. Sponsored by Peaceful Pines. Open to the public.

title: Mother's Tea Party
location: Spearfish Senior Center 1306 N 10th St Spearfish, SD
description: Mother’s Tea Party on May 8th. Tea & refreshments in honor of all our mothers on Friday, May 8th at 1PM. Senior Center Chorus will be performing. Party Hats are not required but encouraged! Free will donation at the door. Open to the public. Invite your family, neighbors, & friends!

title: The Importance of Being Earnest
location: The Matthews Opera House & Art Center
description: Oscar Wilde's The Importance of Being Earnest is coming to The Matthews with crisp pacing, a stylish look, and laughs that land fast. This production keeps Wilde’s razor-sharp dialogue front and center, with active physical comedy, quick turns, and a cast built for speed and chemistry. Expect mistaken identities, secret engagements, and the kind of wit you quote on the way home.

title: The Importance of Being Earnest
location: The Matthews Opera House & Arts Center
description: It’s "High Society Night" for Friday’s performance of The Importance of Being Earnest at The Matthews Opera House. Arrive early for a special pre-show cocktail hour with photo opportunities and extra perks. Dress to impress in classic formal attire (think: an evening at the theatre).

title: The Importance of Being Earnest
location: The Matthews Opera House & Arts Center
description: Oscar Wilde's The Importance of Being Earnest is coming to The Matthews with crisp pacing, a stylish look, and laughs that land fast. This production keeps Wilde’s razor-sharp dialogue front and center, with active physical comedy, quick turns, and a cast built for speed and chemistry. Expect mistaken identities, secret engagements, and the kind of wit you quote on the way home. If you want a “name” comedy that’s pure fun and easy to bring friends to, this is the one.

title: The Importance of Being Earnest
location: The Matthews Opera House & Arts Center
description: Oscar Wilde's The Importance of Being Earnest is coming to The Matthews with crisp pacing, a stylish look, and laughs that land fast. This production keeps Wilde’s razor-sharp dialogue front and center, with active physical comedy, quick turns, and a cast built for speed and chemistry. Expect mistaken identities, secret engagements, and the kind of wit you quote on the way home. If you want a “name” comedy that’s pure fun and easy to bring friends to, this is the one.

title: Ballroom Dance Class
location: The Matthews Opera House & Arts Center
description: Get ready to step onto the dance floor with our Beginner Ballroom Medley lesson series at The Matthews! Designed for absolute beginners (and anyone who wants to refresh the basics), this welcoming four-week series will introduce you to foundational ballroom steps, simple turns, and partner connection across a mix of classic ballroom styles—so you can feel confident at weddings, parties, and social dances. Join instructor LeighAnn Gould for four fun Tuesday-evening classes focused on easy-to-learn patterns, good technique, and a relaxed, encouraging atmosphere. No experience required—just bring a partner and a willingness to try something new (and laugh a little while you learn).

title: Japanese Culture Workshop
location: The Matthews Opera House & Arts Center
description: FREE Manga Workshop Run by the BHSU Japan Outreach Initiative (JOI), this free workshop is limited to the first 12 attendees. All materials included. All workshops begin at 3 pm on the second Thursday of each month and are completely free of charge. The Japan Outreach Initiative (JOI) is a joint program of the Japan Foundation and Laurasian Institution, designed to bring native Japanese cultural coordinators to regions of the U.S. where Japanese cultural experiences are limited. BHSU’s participation brings these rich cultural opportunities right into our community.

title: Creativity on Tap (Sip + Paint)
location: Crow Peak Brewing Company
description: Crow Peak Brewing and The Matthews Opera House invite you to tap into your creativity for an afternoon of paint and sip fun! Open to artists of all skill levels. Follow along in this step-by-step creative workshop to produce a fridge worthy painted piece of perfection. Limited spots.

title: Self Care Sunday
location: The Matthews Opera House & Arts Center
description: Once a month, plan your second Sunday at the Matthews Opera House to escape the day to day hustle. Rest and enjoy the experience of the subtle energies of vibrational healing. Sessions may include light yogic warmups that are gentle and appropriate for all ages and abilities. Bring your yoga mat, water bottle, a fuzzy blanket and any other props to support you as you settle into your most blissful state. Seating is also an option. These times are curated for you to deeply relax and balance your mind/body for the upcoming week. Join us. The class is limited to 30 participants

title: Adult Prom
location: The Matthews Opera House & Arts Center
description: Start planning your promposal now because Prom is coming to The Matthews! Dress in your best high school glam and get ready to boogie! Relive the excitement, glamour, and fun of prom without the stress or expectations. This event transforms our historic opera house into the ultimate throwback dance floor, inviting you to dress up, get creative, and enjoy a carefree night of music and dancing. Post Prom Party at Spearfish Public House after 8pm!

title: Northern Hills Republican Women Monthly mtg
location: VFW, 3102 Fairground Loop, Spearfish
description: Speaker: We welcome members of Mr. Pat Gainey's "We The People" civics class from Spearfish High School. Topic: The students will share their experiences from the 2026 We the People National Finals competition in Washington DC. We the People: The Citizen and the Constitution promotes a greater understanding of our constitutional democracy, its institutions, and its fundamental principles and values. Spearfish High School has offered this curriculum for many years to develop the skills needed to become effective and responsible citizens and to learn how to think rather than what to think. Pizza available for purchase

title: The Two Tracks - Live at The Matthews
location: The Matthews Opera House & Arts Center
description: Rooted in the wide-open landscapes of Wyoming, The Two Tracks create music that fuses rich storytelling, soulful harmonies, and the spirit of the American West. Led by songwriters Julie and Dave Huebner. With Taylor Phillips on bass and Fernando Serna on drums, the band has carved out a sound that nods to ’70s Americana while forging something entirely their own. Dave’s signature cello playing, woven into the band’s four-part harmonies, brings a distinctive edge that sets them apart.

title: 40 Years of Advocacy Celebration
location: The Public House
description: Northern Hills Area CASA proudly celebrates 40 years of advocacy serving children in the Fourth Judicial Circuit, covering eight counties in western South Dakota. Please join us on Sunday, June 7, 2026, from 4:00–7:00 PM at the Public House in Spearfish for an evening of reflection and celebration. The event will feature live music, cake, refreshments, and meaningful testimonials from CASA volunteers, members of the judiciary, attorneys, and youth who have experienced the impact of our program. The Food Addiction Truck will also be on-site with food available for purchase. This celebration is open to the public. We invite the community to join us as we honor four decades of service and express our sincere gratitude for the support that has made this work possible.

title: Children's Theater Auditions
location: The Matthews Opera House & Arts Center
fees: Registration: $50 per child. Paid at time of registration or at auditions (June 8)
description: Join us for auditions for Theatre 605 Tours’ week-long residency production of Space Pirates!—a brand-new children’s musical brought to life by local students and professional touring directors. Auditions are open to students entering 1st through 12th grade (Fall 2026), and no prior experience or preparation is required. Students will participate in fun, group-based auditions led by touring Actor/Directors Nessa Yaakova and Noelle Hall. Casting decisions are made quickly, and all selected performers will rehearse and perform in a full production presented at the end of the week. For more information about registration visit: https://www.matthewsopera.com/childrens-theater-space-pirates/

title: Free Nonprofit Workshop
location: The Joy Center, Spearfish
description: THURSDAY June 11 Mission in Action: Strategies for Planning, Executing, and Evaluating Effective Programs Who is this for? Nonprofit staff and leadership at all levels! Great programs don’t just happen, they’re created with thoughtful planning, clear execution, and evaluation. This interactive workshop walks nonprofit professionals through the entire program journey, from the initial idea all the way to measuring results, by using real-life examples and collaborative activities. Participants will engage in both individual and group exercises that help them apply practical strategies to their own programs. By working together and reflecting on real challenges, attendees will learn how to: Create clear, goal-aligned program plans Improve implementation with simple, effective processes Use data to evaluate impact and tell their program’s story Find ways to strengthen, sustain, and grow their work Use helpful tools and templates to boost program success The focus is on real-world application, so you’ll leave with practical next steps, ready-to-use resources, and greater confidence in running programs that make a meaningful, measurable difference. As always these workshops are FREE to attend and lunch is provided.

title: 2026 YP Social at a Spearfish Sasquatch Game
location: Black Hills Power Sports Complex (Old U.S. 14) Located just off Exit 8 in Spearfish. Look for the light towers and you can't miss it!
fees: 🎟️ $20 per ticket for first 40 to register - 🎟️ $38 per ticket after first 40 spots are filled Includes entry into the game, VIP patio access, & all-you-can-eat food and beverages
description: Join The Hook Young Professionals for a Spearfish Sasquatch baseball game! **Register by June 10** Join The Hook - Spearfish Chamber Young Professionals for an all-inclusive Spearfish Sasquatch experience! Get your tickets right away because for the first 40 to register it is almost HALF OFF! $20 for the first 40. $38 after 40 spots are filled. Your ticket includes entry into the game, VIP patio access, & all-you-can-eat food and beverages. Gates open at 5:05 PM. Game begins at 6:05 PM. At Black Hills Power Sports Complex (Old U.S. 14) - Located just off Exit 8 in Spearfish. Look for the light towers and you can't miss it! You must register using the link above, and your tickets will be held at will call. Let's go wild with the Spearfish Sasquatch! —— Thank you to The Hook's 2026 sponsors: Black Hills Energy | Blind Guy of the Black Hills | Bluepeak | Coeur Mining | Lighthouse Construction | Platinum Car Brokers | SDN Communications | Gregg Fullerton - State Farm Agent | Sundance State Bank | Wolff's Plumbing & Heating
